financial.en: relating to money or the management of money

ex.en: The company is going through financial difficulties.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 재정적인 어려움을 겪고 있다.

finding.en: something discovered or learned through research or investigation

ex.en: The study's findings surprised the researchers.
ex.ko: 그 연구의 결과는 연구자들을 놀라게 했다.

finger.en: one of the five long parts at the end of the hand

ex.en: She pointed at the map with her finger.

fire station.en: a building where firefighters and fire engines are based

ex.en: The fire station is just around the corner.

firefighter.en: a person whose job is to put out fires

ex.en: The firefighters rescued a cat from the tree.

firework.en: a device that explodes in the sky to produce bright colors and light, used for celebrations

ex.en: We watched the fireworks on New Year's Eve.

firm.en: having a solid, definite quality; not easily moved or changed

ex.en: She gave a firm handshake.
He works for a law firm.

firm.en: become or make firm or solid

ex.en: Regular exercise helps firm up your muscles.
ex.ko: 꾸준한 운동은 근육을 단단하게 만드는 데 도움이 된다.

firmly.en: in a strong and determined way

ex.en: He firmly believes in equal rights.

first floor.en: the floor of a building at ground level

ex.en: The lobby is on the first floor.

first lady.en: the wife of a president or head of state

ex.en: The First Lady attended the charity event.
We booked a first-floor room with a garden view.

fitness.en: the condition of being physically strong and healthy

ex.en: She goes to the gym to improve her fitness.
Lightning flashed across the sky.

flash.en: a sudden brief burst of light

ex.en: There was a flash of lightning before the thunder.

flash.en: a device that produces a bright light for taking photos

ex.en: Don't use the flash indoors, it's too bright.
ex.ko: 실내에서는 플래시를 쓰지 마세요, 너무 밝아요.

ex.en: A memory flashed through her mind.
ex.ko: 어떤 기억이 그녀의 머릿속을 스치고 지나갔다.

flavor.en: the specific taste of a food or drink

ex.en: This ice cream has a strong vanilla flavor.

flavor.en: give a particular taste to food or drink

ex.en: The soup was flavored with garlic and thyme.
ex.ko: 그 수프는 마늘과 타임으로 맛을 냈다.

flavor.en: a distinctive quality or atmosphere of something

ex.en: The old town has a unique flavor.
ex.ko: 그 구시가지는 독특한 분위기를 가지고 있다.

flock.en: a group of birds or animals such as sheep, that stay or move together

ex.en: A flock of birds flew over the field.

flock.en: gather or move somewhere in a large group

ex.en: Tourists flock to the beach in summer.

flow.en: move steadily and continuously in one direction

ex.en: The river flows through the valley.
There was a steady flow of traffic on the highway.

flu.en: an illness like a bad cold that causes fever and body aches

ex.en: I caught the flu last week.

fluent.en: able to speak or write a language easily and accurately

ex.en: She is fluent in three languages.

fluently.en: in a way that shows you can speak or write a language easily and well

ex.en: He speaks English fluently.

flunk.en: fail a test, course, or exam

ex.en: He flunked his math exam.

flute.en: a thin metal musical instrument that you play by blowing across a hole

ex.en: She practices the flute every afternoon.

flute.en: a tall narrow glass used for drinking champagne

ex.en: They toasted with champagne flutes.

fold.en: bend something so that one part covers another part

ex.en: She folded the letter and put it in an envelope.

fold.en: a line or crease made by folding something

ex.en: There were deep folds in the curtain.

fold.en: (of a business) fail and stop operating

ex.en: The small bakery folded after just two years.
ex.ko: 그 작은 빵집은 겨우 2년 만에 문을 닫았다.

folk.en: people in general, used in an informal way

ex.en: Folks around here are very friendly.

folk.en: traditional, relating to the culture and art of ordinary people

ex.en: She grew up listening to folk music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어릴 때부터 민속 음악을 들으며 자랐다.

fond.en: having a liking or affection for someone or something

ex.en: She is very fond of her grandmother.

fondness.en: a feeling of liking someone or something

ex.en: He has a fondness for old movies.
ex.ko: 그는 오래된 영화에 애정을 가지고 있다.

foolish.en: showing a lack of good sense or judgment

ex.en: It was foolish of me to trust him.
ex.ko: 그를 믿었던 것은 나의 어리석은 짓이었다.

foot.en: the part of the body at the end of the leg that you stand on

ex.en: She hurt her foot playing soccer.

foot.en: a unit of length equal to 12 inches (about 30 cm)

ex.en: The room is ten feet wide.

foot.en: pay for something, especially a bill

ex.en: The company agreed to foot the bill for the repairs.
ex.ko: 그 회사가 수리 비용을 부담하기로 했다.

forbidden.en: not allowed by rule or law

ex.en: Smoking is forbidden in this building.
ex.ko: 이 건물 안에서는 흡연이 금지되어 있다.

forecast.en: a statement about what is expected to happen in the future, especially the weather

ex.en: The forecast says it will rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 예보에 따르면 내일 비가 온다고 한다.

forecast.en: say what you expect to happen in the future

ex.en: Experts forecast a rise in prices next year.
ex.ko: 전문가들은 내년에 물가가 오를 것이라고 예측한다.

forehead.en: the part of the face above the eyes and below the hair

ex.en: He wiped the sweat from his forehead.

forgive.en: stop feeling angry with someone for something they did wrong

ex.en: I forgave him for being late.

formal.en: following accepted rules and conventions, especially in dress or manner

ex.en: You should wear formal clothes to the interview.
ex.ko: 면접에는 격식을 차린 옷을 입어야 한다.

formally.en: in an official or formal way

ex.en: The new policy was formally announced yesterday.

format.en: the way something is arranged or organized

ex.en: The report follows a standard format.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 표준 서식을 따르고 있다.

format.en: arrange text or data according to a specific plan, or prepare a disk for storing data

ex.en: You need to format the new hard drive first.
ex.ko: 먼저 새 하드 드라이브를 포맷해야 한다.

former.en: having been something in the past but not now

ex.en: She is the former president of the company.

former.cue: 둘 중 먼저 언급한 것을 가리켜
former.en: the first of two things or people just mentioned

ex.en: Of the two options, I prefer the former.
ex.ko: 그 두 선택지 중에서 나는 전자를 선호한다.

formula.en: a set method or plan for doing or making something, especially in math or science

ex.en: Scientists developed a new formula for the vaccine.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 백신을 위한 새로운 공식을 개발했다.

formula.en: a liquid food given to babies instead of breast milk

ex.en: The baby drinks formula every few hours.
ex.ko: 그 아기는 몇 시간마다 분유를 먹는다.

forth.en: forward or onward in time, place, or order

ex.en: From that day forth, everything changed.
We're going on vacation for a fortnight.
ex.ko: 우리는 보름 동안 휴가를 갈 것이다.

ex.en: We were fortunate to find a parking spot.
ex.ko: 우리는 운 좋게 주차할 자리를 찾았다.

foundation.en: the basic ideas or structure that something is built on or depends on

ex.en: Hard work is the foundation of success.

foundation.en: an organization that provides money for research, education, or charity

ex.en: The foundation supports education projects around the world.
ex.ko: 그 재단은 전 세계 교육 사업을 지원한다.

foundation.en: a makeup product used to even out and cover the skin

ex.en: She applied foundation before putting on the rest of her makeup.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나머지 화장을 하기 전에 파운데이션을 발랐다.

fountain.en: a structure that shoots water into the air, often as a decoration

ex.en: Children threw coins into the fountain.

fragment.en: a small piece broken off or separated from something bigger

ex.en: She stepped on a fragment of broken glass.

fragment.en: break or separate into small pieces

ex.en: The vase fragmented into hundreds of pieces when it fell.
ex.ko: 그 꽃병은 떨어지면서 수백 개의 조각으로 산산조각 났다.

ex.en: Visitors can move freely around the museum.
ex.ko: 방문객들은 박물관을 자유롭게 돌아다닐 수 있다.

freezer.en: a machine or part of a refrigerator that keeps food frozen

ex.en: Put the ice cream back in the freezer.

freezing.en: extremely cold, at or below the temperature at which water turns to ice

ex.en: It's freezing outside, wear a warm coat.
ex.ko: 밖이 몹시 추우니 따뜻한 코트를 입어라.

frequency.en: how often something happens over a period of time

ex.en: The frequency of buses increases during rush hour.

frequency.en: the rate at which a sound wave or radio signal vibrates

ex.en: Radio stations broadcast on different frequencies.
ex.ko: 라디오 방송국들은 서로 다른 주파수로 방송한다.

ex.en: She is a frequent visitor to this cafe.
He frequents the local coffee shop every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 동네 커피숍에 자주 간다.

ex.en: They frequently argue about small things.

freshman.en: a student in the first year of high school or college

ex.en: She just started as a freshman at college.

friendliness.en: the quality of being kind and pleasant to other people

ex.en: The staff greeted us with great friendliness.

fright.en: a sudden strong feeling of fear

ex.en: The loud noise gave her a real fright.
ex.ko: 그 큰 소리가 그녀에게 정말로 공포를 안겼다.

frost.en: a thin white layer of ice that forms outdoors when it is very cold

ex.en: There was frost on the grass this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 잔디에 서리가 내려 있었다.

frost.en: cover a cake with a sweet, creamy coating

ex.en: She frosted the cake with pink icing.

frost.en: cover something with a thin layer of frost

ex.en: The windshield was frosted over this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 자동차 앞유리가 서리로 덮여 있었다.

frozen.en: turned into ice, or covered with ice

ex.en: The lake was completely frozen in January.

frozen.en: (of food) preserved by keeping it very cold

ex.en: We had frozen pizza for dinner.

frozen.en: unable to move because of fear or shock

ex.en: She stood frozen with fear when she saw the snake.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뱀을 보고 두려움에 얼어붙어 서 있었다.

frustrated.en: feeling annoyed or upset because you cannot achieve or do something

ex.en: He felt frustrated when the internet kept disconnecting.
ex.ko: 그는 인터넷이 계속 끊겨서 답답함을 느꼈다.

frustration.en: the feeling of being annoyed or upset because you cannot achieve what you want

ex.en: She sighed in frustration at the long line.
ex.ko: 그녀는 긴 줄에 좌절하며 한숨을 쉬었다.

frying pan.en: a shallow flat pan used for frying food

ex.en: He cracked an egg into the frying pan.

fuel.en: a substance such as gas or oil that is burned to produce energy

ex.en: The car ran out of fuel on the highway.

fuel.en: supply with fuel, or make a feeling or situation stronger

ex.en: The scandal fueled public anger.

full stop.en: the punctuation mark (.) used at the end of a sentence

ex.en: Don't forget to put a full stop at the end.
ex.ko: 끝에 마침표 찍는 것 잊지 마세요.

full-time.en: working for the whole of the usual working week, not part of it

ex.en: She has a full-time job at the hospital.

fund.en: an amount of money saved or collected for a particular purpose

ex.en: The school set up a fund for scholarships.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 장학금을 위한 기금을 마련했다.

fund.en: provide money for a project or activity

ex.en: The government funds several research programs.
ex.ko: 정부는 여러 연구 프로그램에 자금을 지원한다.

funeral.en: a ceremony held for a person who has died, usually before burial or cremation

ex.en: Many friends and family attended the funeral.

fur.en: the soft thick hair that covers the bodies of some animals

ex.en: The cat's fur was soft and warm.

furnish.en: provide a room or house with furniture

ex.en: They furnished the apartment with secondhand furniture.
ex.ko: 그들은 중고 가구로 그 아파트를 꾸몄다.

furnish.en: supply or provide something that is needed

ex.en: Please furnish us with the necessary documents.

furthermore.en: in addition to what has already been said

ex.en: The plan is expensive; furthermore, it may not even work.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 비용이 많이 든다. 게다가 효과가 없을 수도 있다.

furthest.en: to the greatest distance or degree (superlative of far)

ex.en: Of all the runners, she went the furthest.
ex.ko: 모든 주자 중에서 그녀가 가장 멀리 갔다.

fuss.en: unnecessary excitement, worry, or complaints about something small

ex.en: There's no need to make such a fuss over a small mistake.
ex.ko: 사소한 실수에 그렇게 야단법석을 떨 필요는 없다.

fuss.en: worry or behave in an anxious or excited way about small things

ex.en: Stop fussing over such a minor mistake.
ex.ko: 그런 사소한 실수에 유난 떨지 마.

gain.en: obtain or win something, especially through effort

ex.en: She gained valuable experience from the internship.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 인턴십을 통해 소중한 경험을 얻었다.

gain.en: an increase in amount, value, or quality

ex.en: The company reported a gain in profits this quarter.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이번 분기에 이익 증가를 보고했다.

ex.en: He gained five kilograms over the winter.

gall.en: rude boldness or confidence, especially when doing something inappropriate

ex.en: He had the gall to ask for a raise after being late all week.
ex.ko: 그는 일주일 내내 지각하고도 뻔뻔하게 임금 인상을 요구했다.

gallon.en: a unit for measuring liquid, equal to about 3.8 liters in the US

ex.en: The tank holds ten gallons of gas.
ex.ko: 그 탱크는 10갤런의 기름을 담을 수 있다.

gap.en: an empty space or opening between two things

ex.en: There's a gap in the fence where the dog escaped.

gap.en: a difference between two situations, amounts, or ideas

ex.en: There's a big gap between rich and poor in that country.

gardening.en: the activity of growing and taking care of plants in a garden

ex.en: She spends every weekend gardening.
Each garment is carefully checked before shipping.
ex.ko: 각 의류는 배송 전에 꼼꼼히 검사된다.

gay.en: attracted to people of the same sex

ex.en: He came out as gay in college.
ex.ko: 그는 대학 시절 자신이 동성애자임을 밝혔다.

gay.en: cheerful and full of fun (an older use of the word)

ex.en: The town had a gay, festive atmosphere during the carnival.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 축제 기간 동안 명랑하고 흥겨운 분위기였다.

gee.en: used to express surprise, enthusiasm, or mild annoyance

ex.en: Gee, I didn't expect to see you here!
ex.ko: 와, 여기서 널 볼 줄은 몰랐어!

gene.en: a unit of DNA that determines a particular characteristic passed from parents to children

ex.en: Eye color is determined by genes.

general.en: true or applicable in most cases, not specific

ex.en: This is a general rule that applies to most situations.
ex.ko: 이것은 대부분의 상황에 적용되는 일반적인 규칙이다.

general.en: a senior military officer of high rank

ex.en: The general led the troops into battle.
People generally prefer to work close to home.
ex.ko: 사람들은 일반적으로 집 가까이서 일하는 것을 선호한다.

ex.en: Solar panels generate electricity from sunlight.

generous.en: willing to give more money, help, or time than is expected

ex.en: He is always generous with his time.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 자신의 시간을 아끼지 않고 베푼다.

ex.en: The disease has a genetic cause.

genetically.en: in a way relating to genes or heredity

ex.en: The crop was genetically modified to resist disease.
ex.ko: 그 작물은 병에 저항하도록 유전적으로 변형되었다.

genetics.en: the scientific study of genes and heredity

ex.en: She is studying genetics at university.
He behaved like a true gentleman.

geographical.en: relating to geography or the physical features of a place

ex.en: The two countries are close in geographical terms.

geography.en: the study of the earth's physical features and how people live in different places

ex.en: We are studying the geography of South America.

geology.en: the scientific study of rocks and the physical structure of the earth

ex.en: He majored in geology at college.

gesture.en: a movement of the hands, arms, or head that expresses a meaning or feeling

ex.en: He made a gesture for her to come closer.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 가까이 오라고 몸짓을 했다.

gesture.en: make a movement with your hands or head to express something

ex.en: She gestured toward the door.

gesture.en: an action done to show a feeling such as kindness, respect, or gratitude

ex.en: Sending flowers was a kind gesture.
ex.ko: 꽃을 보낸 것은 친절한 마음의 표시였다.

ex.en: A giant billboard stood at the entrance to the city.
ex.ko: 도시 입구에 거대한 광고판이 서 있었다.

giant.cue: 이야기 속의 몸집이 아주 큰 존재
giant.en: a person or thing of unusually great size, power, or importance

ex.en: He is a giant in the world of technology.

gifted.en: having a natural ability or talent

ex.en: She is a gifted musician.

giggle.en: laugh quietly in a nervous or silly way

ex.en: The girls giggled at the funny video.
ex.ko: 그 소녀들은 재미있는 영상을 보고 낄낄 웃었다.

giggle.en: a quiet, nervous, or silly laugh

ex.en: She let out a little giggle.

ginger.en: a plant root with a spicy taste, used in cooking and medicine

ex.en: She added fresh ginger to the tea.

giraffe.en: a tall African animal with a very long neck

ex.en: The giraffe reached up to eat leaves from the tree.
ex.ko: 그 기린은 나무 잎을 먹으려고 목을 뻗었다.

glance.en: look quickly and briefly at something

ex.en: She glanced at her watch during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 시계를 힐끗 봤다.

ex.en: He gave her a quick glance and walked away.

glide.en: move smoothly and quietly, without effort

ex.en: The swan glided across the lake.
ex.ko: 그 백조는 호수 위를 미끄러지듯 나아갔다.

glide.en: a smooth flight without engine power

ex.en: The plane went into a long glide before landing.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 착륙 전 긴 활공에 들어갔다.

glimpse.en: a brief or incomplete view of something

ex.en: I caught a glimpse of her through the crowd.
ex.ko: 나는 사람들 사이로 그녀를 잠깐 봤다.

glimpse.en: see something for a very short time

ex.en: She glimpsed the ocean between the buildings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 건물 사이로 바다를 언뜻 보았다.

glint.en: a small, brief flash of light

ex.en: There was a glint of gold in the sand.

glint.en: shine briefly with small flashes of light

ex.en: Her eyes glinted with excitement.

global.en: involving or affecting the whole world

ex.en: Climate change is a global problem.

glorious.en: wonderful, impressive, or beautiful, and deserving admiration

ex.en: It was a glorious sunset over the mountains.

glory.en: great honor, praise, or beauty that comes from an important achievement

ex.en: The team celebrated their moment of glory.

goalkeeper.en: the player who stands in the goal and tries to stop the other team from scoring

ex.en: The goalkeeper made an amazing save.

goddess.en: a female god in a myth or religion

ex.en: Athena was the Greek goddess of wisdom.
She is one of the top golfers in the country.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 나라 최고의 골프 선수 중 한 명이다.

goodness.en: the quality of being good, kind, or morally right

ex.en: She believes in the basic goodness of people.

goodness.en: used to express surprise (as in 'my goodness')

ex.en: Goodness, that was fast!

goods.en: things that are made or produced to be sold

ex.en: The store sells a wide variety of goods.
She looked gorgeous in that red dress.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 빨간 드레스를 입으니 정말 아름다웠다.

ex.en: We watched the gorillas at the zoo.

gossip.en: talk about other people's private lives, often unkindly

ex.en: She loves gossiping about her coworkers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동료들에 대해 험담하는 것을 좋아한다.

gossip.en: casual talk about other people's private affairs

ex.en: Don't believe all the gossip you hear.

govern.en: officially control and lead a country or area

ex.en: The country is governed by an elected parliament.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 선출된 의회에 의해 통치된다.

governor.en: the person who officially leads a state or region

ex.en: The governor announced new safety measures.
ex.ko: 그 주지사는 새로운 안전 조치를 발표했다.

gown.cue: 격식 있는 자리에서 입는 긴 옷
gown.en: a long, formal dress worn by women, or a loose garment worn for a specific purpose

ex.en: She wore an elegant gown to the wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결혼식에 우아한 드레스를 입고 왔다.

grab.en: take hold of something suddenly and firmly

ex.en: She grabbed her bag and ran out the door.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가방을 붙잡고 문 밖으로 뛰어나갔다.

grab.en: take or get something quickly, especially an opportunity or a quick meal

ex.en: Let's grab a coffee before the meeting.
ex.ko: 회의 전에 커피 한 잔 얼른 마시자.

graceful.en: moving in a smooth and attractive way

ex.en: The dancer moved with graceful steps.

graduation.en: the completion of a course of study, often marked by a ceremony

ex.en: Her whole family came to her graduation.

grant.en: a sum of money given by an organization for a particular purpose

ex.en: She received a grant to fund her research.
ex.ko: 그녀는 연구 자금을 위한 지원금을 받았다.

grant.en: agree to give someone what they have asked for

ex.en: The judge granted the request.

graph.en: a diagram showing the relationship between different amounts or numbers

ex.en: The graph shows sales over the past year.

graphic.en: relating to visual art, drawings, or computer images

ex.en: She works as a graphic designer.
The book contains graphic descriptions of the war.
ex.ko: 그 책에는 전쟁에 대한 적나라한 묘사가 담겨 있다.

graphics.en: images or visual designs, especially those made by a computer

ex.en: The video game has amazing graphics.

gratitude.en: a feeling of thankfulness and appreciation

ex.en: She expressed her gratitude for their help.

grave.en: a place in the ground where a dead person is buried

ex.en: They placed flowers on the grave.

grave.en: very serious or important, often causing worry

ex.en: The doctor's expression was grave.

gravy.en: a sauce made from the juices of cooked meat

ex.en: She poured gravy over the mashed potatoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 으깬 감자 위에 그레이비 소스를 부었다.

greenhouse.en: a glass building used for growing plants

ex.en: They grow tomatoes in a greenhouse all year round.
ex.ko: 그들은 온실에서 일 년 내내 토마토를 재배한다.

greeting.en: a word or action used when meeting or welcoming someone

ex.en: He gave a warm greeting to his guests.

grill.en: cook food on a metal frame over direct heat

ex.en: We grilled some steaks for dinner.

grill.en: a metal frame used for cooking food over direct heat

ex.en: They set up the grill in the backyard.
The police grilled the suspect for hours.
ex.ko: 경찰은 몇 시간 동안 용의자를 심문했다.

groom.en: a man on the day of his wedding

ex.en: The groom looked nervous before the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그 신랑은 식이 시작되기 전에 긴장한 모습이었다.

groom.en: take care of your appearance, or take care of an animal's coat

ex.en: She spent an hour grooming her hair before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전에 한 시간 동안 머리를 손질했다.

ground floor.en: the floor of a building at ground level

ex.en: The reception desk is on the ground floor.

grouping.en: a set of things or people arranged together into a group

ex.en: The students were divided into groupings based on skill level.
ex.ko: 학생들은 실력 수준에 따라 그룹으로 나뉘었다.

growth.en: the process of increasing in size, number, value, or importance

ex.en: The company has seen steady growth over the past year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 지난 1년간 꾸준한 성장을 보였다.

guarantee.en: promise that something will definitely happen or be true

ex.en: We can't guarantee the weather will be good.
ex.ko: 우리는 날씨가 좋을 거라고 보장할 수 없다.

guarantee.en: a written promise that a product will be repaired or replaced if faulty

ex.en: The product comes with a one-year guarantee.

guidance.en: help or advice that guides someone's decisions or actions

ex.en: She gave her students career guidance.

guide.en: show someone the way or help them do something

ex.en: The teacher guided the students through the experiment.

guide.en: a person who shows others the way or gives them information

ex.en: Our guide took us through the old town.

guide.en: a book that gives instructions or information about a place or subject

ex.en: She bought a travel guide for Japan.

guilt.en: the uncomfortable feeling of having done something wrong

ex.en: He felt guilt over forgetting her birthday.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 생일을 잊은 것에 죄책감을 느꼈다.

ex.en: The jury found him guilty of theft.

guilty.en: feeling bad because you have done something wrong

ex.en: I feel guilty for forgetting her birthday.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 생일을 잊어서 죄책감을 느낀다.

guitarist.en: a person who plays the guitar

ex.en: He is the lead guitarist in the band.

gym.en: a place with equipment for exercise and physical training

ex.en: She goes to the gym three times a week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일주일에 세 번 헬스장에 간다.

gymnastics.en: a sport involving exercises that show strength, balance, and flexibility

ex.en: She has practiced gymnastics since she was five.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다섯 살 때부터 체조를 해왔다.

habitat.en: the natural environment where an animal or plant normally lives

ex.en: Deforestation destroys the habitat of many animals.
ex.ko: 삼림 벌채는 많은 동물의 서식지를 파괴한다.

hairdresser.en: a person whose job is to cut and style hair

ex.en: I have an appointment with the hairdresser this afternoon.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 오후에 미용사와 예약이 있다.

hairdryer.en: an electric device used to dry hair by blowing warm air

ex.en: She used a hairdryer to dry her hair quickly.

hammer.en: a tool with a heavy metal head used for hitting nails

ex.en: He used a hammer to fix the shelf.

hammer.en: hit something repeatedly with a hammer or with force

ex.en: He hammered the nail into the wall.

hand-held.en: small enough to be held and used in the hand

ex.en: She used a hand-held device to scan the tickets.
ex.ko: 그녀는 티켓을 스캔하는 데 휴대용 기기를 사용했다.

handball.en: a sport in which two teams throw a ball into the other team's goal using their hands

ex.en: They play handball every Friday after school.
ex.ko: 그들은 매주 금요일 방과 후에 핸드볼을 한다.

handkerchief.en: a small square piece of cloth used to wipe the nose or face

ex.en: He offered her a handkerchief to dry her tears.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 눈물을 닦으라고 손수건을 건넸다.

handshake.en: the act of grasping and shaking someone's hand as a greeting or agreement

ex.en: They sealed the deal with a handshake.

handwriting.en: writing done by hand, or the style of a person's writing

ex.en: Her handwriting is neat and easy to read.

handy.en: useful, especially for a particular purpose

ex.en: This tool is handy for small repairs.
ex.ko: 이 도구는 작은 수리 작업에 편리하다.

handy.cue: 필요할 때 바로 쓸 수 있게 가까이

ex.en: Keep a first aid kit handy in the car.

hang.en: the correct way of doing or using something (usually 'get the hang of')

ex.en: It took me a while to get the hang of the new software.
ex.ko: 새 소프트웨어의 요령을 익히는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

hang.en: attach something so that the lower part is free and it does not touch the ground

ex.en: She hung the picture on the wall.

hang.en: kill someone by suspending them by a rope around the neck

ex.en: The criminal was sentenced to hang.

hanging.en: a decorative piece of cloth or artwork that is hung on a wall

ex.en: They bought a colorful wall hanging from the market.
ex.ko: 그들은 시장에서 화려한 벽걸이 장식을 샀다.

hanging.en: a method of execution by hanging someone by the neck

ex.en: Hanging was once a common form of punishment.

harbor.en: a place along the coast where ships can shelter and dock safely

ex.en: The boats returned to the harbor before the storm.
ex.ko: 배들은 폭풍이 오기 전에 항구로 돌아왔다.

harbor.en: keep a feeling or thought in your mind for a long time

ex.en: She harbored doubts about the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획에 대해 의구심을 품고 있었다.

hard-working.en: putting a lot of effort and energy into work

ex.en: She is one of the most hard-working employees in the office.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사무실에서 가장 근면한 직원 중 한 명이다.

hardship.en: a difficult or painful situation that is hard to deal with

ex.en: The family faced many hardships during the war.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 전쟁 동안 많은 고난을 겪었다.
